title: AutoLogon enabled for an unexpected user
description: Optional kiosk allow-list for AutoLogon. Fires when AutoLogon is enabled for a default user that is NOT on your configured list of approved kiosk / shared-device accounts. Disabled until configured because a normal Autopilot enrollment leaves Windows' own ESP auto-logon for the enrolling user (AutoLogon enabled, a default user, no plaintext password) — so AutoLogon-enabled alone is not graded by default; only a plaintext DefaultPassword on disk (ANALYZE-SEC-003) is. Enable this rule with your expected kiosk account name(s) if you want to surface AutoLogon configured for any other user.
explanation:
AutoLogon is enabled on this device for a user that is **not on your approved kiosk allow-list**. AutoLogon is legitimate for kiosk and shared-device scenarios, so this rule only warns when the configured default user differs from the account(s) you expect.

This rule is **off by default**. A normal user-driven Autopilot enrollment leaves Windows' own ESP auto-logon for the enrolling user (`AutoAdminLogon=1`, a `DefaultUserName`, an `AutoLogonSID`, and no plaintext password) — registry-indistinguishable from a kiosk AutoLogon — so AutoLogon-enabled alone is not graded. The only AutoLogon state graded by default is a plaintext `DefaultPassword` on disk (**ANALYZE-SEC-003**).

To use this rule: configure the allow-list with your approved kiosk account name(s) (comma-separated, case-insensitive exact match against the Winlogon `DefaultUserName`) and enable it. AutoLogon for any other user will then warn.

Note: an AutoLogon with no explicit default user (e.g. ForceAutoLogon only) is treated as unexpected and will warn.

remediation.title: Confirm the AutoLogon user
steps: Open the autologon_analysis event in the session timeline and check the default user/domain
If the user is an approved kiosk account, add it to this rule's allow-list
If the user is unexpected, treat it as a possible enrollment/OOBE manipulation and investigate what wrote the Winlogon keys

remediation.title: Disable AutoLogon if not required
steps: Remove DefaultPassword (and any Sysinternals LSA secret) if present
For kiosk scenarios, prefer Assigned Access / kiosk profiles over raw AutoLogon where possible

relatedDocs.title: Microsoft: Configure Windows to automatically sign in (AutoLogon)

templateVariables.name: allowed_autologon_users
templateVariables.label: Allowed AutoLogon users (kiosk accounts)
templateVariables.description: Comma-separated list of approved kiosk / shared-device account names that are expected to use AutoLogon (case-insensitive exact match against the Winlogon DefaultUserName, e.g. 'KioskUser' or 'Reception,LobbyPC'). AutoLogon for any other user raises a warning.
